title: sedum obtusipetalum subsp. dandyanum
intro: Sedum obtusipetalum subsp. dandyanum, commonly known as Dandy's stonecrop, is a subspecies of flowering succulent plant in the family Crassulaceae. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Philippines, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal effects. The health benefits of Dandy's stonecrop include its potential to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antidote for snake bites. Dandy's stonecrop is often prepared as a decoction or infusion by boiling the leaves and stems in water, and it is also used in herbal preparations such as topical creams and ointments to treat skin conditions.
